original: ARTICLE I   [Term] Section 1.01. Term. The term of this Agreement shall be effective as of the date first stated above and shall continue for a term of three (3) years, unless terminated earlier in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement (the "Term"); provided, however, that PrimeCall may elect to terminate this Agreement, upon thirty (30) days' written notice, at any time from and after the time that collectively RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates holds less than fifty percent (50%) of the voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. "Affiliate" as used in this Agreement shall mean any person directly or indirectly controlling or controlled by or under direct or indirect common control with such person.
ollama_generated:
ARTICLE I [Term] Section 1.01. Term. This Agreement shall commence on the date first stated above and shall remain in effect for a term of three (3) years, subject to early termination as outlined within this Agreement (hereinafter referred to as "the Term"). Notably, DeltaThree retains the option to terminate this Agreement, effective thirty (30) days following written notification, should PrimeCall no longer hold ownership of more than fifty percent (50%) of RSL Communications, Ltd.'s or its Affiliates' voting control of DeltaThree's outstanding shares. In this context, "Affiliate" denotes any person who exercises direct or indirect control over, is controlled by, or is under common control with such person, as defined within the terms of this Agreement.

In this revised version, the obligation to terminate the agreement upon certain conditions has been shifted from PrimeCall to DeltaThree, and the condition for termination has been slightly altered to require DeltaThree to hold less than 50% of voting control by PrimeCall or its affiliates instead of RSL Communications, Ltd. and/or its Affiliates. The language remains professional and clear while altering the responsibilities and conditions outlined in the original clause.

original: Section 3.06   [Reporting] . Reporting. On or before the tenth day of each calendar month, PrimeCall shall prepare for DeltaThree a report detailing the total revenues and expenses associated with the sale and use of the Calling Cards in the previous month. In the event that DeltaThree disputes any amounts set forth on the report, DeltaThree shall provide PrimeCall with written notice of such dispute, which notice shall detail the reasons for the dispute, within seven (7) days of its receipt thereof. The parties shall negotiate in good faith to resolve any such disputes.
ollama_generated:
Section 3.06 [Report Submission]: Reporting Obligations

PrimeCall is responsible for preparing and submitting, no later than the tenth day of each calendar month, a report outlining the total revenues and expenses related to the sale and utilization of Calling Cards from the preceding month, addressed to DeltaThree. In the event that PrimeCall contests any figures presented in the report, PrimeCall shall deliver DeltaThree written notice of the dispute, including grounds for contestation, within seven (7) days of receiving the report. The parties are expected to engage in good faith negotiations to resolve any ensuing disputes.

In this revised clause, the obligation for preparing and submitting reports has been shifted from DeltaThree to PrimeCall. Additionally, the condition for dispute resolution is inverted so that PrimeCall is now the one providing written notice of a dispute, if applicable.

ollama_generated:
Section 4.02 - Reporting Obligations:

DeltaThree is obligated to submit, on or before the tenth day of each calendar month, a detailed report to PrimeCall regarding the total revenues and expenses associated with the sale of PrimeCall's calling cards via the World Wide Web for the preceding month. This report shall be prepared by DeltaThree for PrimeCall's review.

In case PrimeCall disputes any amounts stated in the report, DeltaThree shall have the responsibility to address and resolve such disputes within seven (7) days of receiving written notice from PrimeCall, which notice shall specify the grounds for the dispute. The parties are expected to engage in good faith negotiations to settle any disagreements that may arise.

In this revised clause, DeltaThree now bears the responsibility to address and resolve disputes, shifting the initial obligation from PrimeCall to DeltaThree.

ollama_generated:
Section 9.03 - Notifications

Notifications: All notices or communications mandated or permitted pursuant to this agreement shall be in writing and will be deemed effectively transmitted upon:

1. Personal delivery or dispatch via private courier.
2. Mail delivery through registered or certified United States mail, with return receipt requested and prepaid postage, except that notice shall be considered delivered three business days after the date of mailing if the recipient does not acknowledge receipt within that timeframe.
3. Electronic transmission (subject to verification via email confirmation), provided the recipient has agreed to electronic communication as a valid means of notice. In such cases, addresses for delivery shall be as follows:

For the sender: [Recipient's information]
For the recipient: [Sender's information]

If by DeltaThree:

In the event that DeltaThree fails to fulfill its obligations under this agreement, it shall be liable for any resulting damages or losses incurred by the other party, subject to the terms and conditions outlined herein.

original: Section 9.07   [Entire Agreement] . Entire Agreement. All-Inclusive Agreement. This Agreement, along with any accompanying documents to be exchanged hereunder, constitutes the full extent of the agreement and comprehension between the parties concerning the subject matter herein. original: --------------------------------- Name: Amos Sela Title:  President and CEO Source: DELTATHREE INC, S-1/A, 11/2/1999
ollama_generated:
Clause:
--------------------------------- Name: Dr. Amos Sela Position: Executive Chairperson and Chief Operating Officer Source: THREEDELTA CORP., Registration Statement S-1/A, November 2, 1999

In the event that our company experiences a change in control (as defined below), the obligations set forth in this Agreement shall be assignable by us only to the successor entity that:

(a) assumes all or substantially all of our assets and liabilities;
(b) consents in writing to be bound by the terms of this Agreement; and
(c) agrees to perform this Agreement's obligations as fully as we would have been required to perform if no such change in control had occurred (except that references to "we," "us," and "our" shall mean the successor entity).

For clarity, a "change in control" of our company shall be deemed to have occurred upon the occurrence of any of the following events:

(i) any Person becomes the beneficial owner (as defined below), directly or indirectly, of securities representing 50% or more of the combined voting power of our company's then outstanding securities;
(ii) during any period of three consecutive years, individuals who constitute our board of directors cease for any reason to constitute at least a majority thereof; provided, however, that any individual becoming a director subsequent to the effective date of this Agreement shall, for purposes of this clause (ii), be considered as part of the board of directors in office on such effective date; or
(iii) our company merges with or into another corporation, other than a merger which results in no more than approximately 50% of the combined voting power of the surviving entity's then outstanding securities being represented by Persons who were stockholders of our company immediately prior to such merger.

For purposes of this definition, "Person" means an individual or entity (including a "group" as defined in Rule 13d-3 promulgated under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended), and "beneficial owner" shall have the meaning given to such term in Rule 13d-3.

By way of example, a change in control would not be deemed to have occurred solely because (a) securities representing more than 50% of the combined voting power are acquired by an investor with no present intention of changing management or operational control of our company; (b) any Person acquires securities of our company in a manner that does not result in such Person being deemed to have beneficial ownership of such securities for purposes of this clause; or (c) any Person, including Amos Sela, is elected to our board of directors as a result of an extraordinary corporate transaction.

The foregoing provisions of this section shall be exclusively governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware (without giving effect to its principles regarding conflicts of law). This Agreement may not be amended except by an instrument in writing signed by us and the other party hereto. Any term or provision of this Agreement that is invalid or becomes invalid under any applicable law shall be interpreted so as best to accomplish the objectives of such term or provision to the fullest extent possible under applicable law, and the remaining terms and provisions of this Agreement shall not be affected by the invalidity of such term or provision. This clause supersedes any prior definition or description of a change in control contained herein or in any other agreement between us.
